title: Roadmap: jjforge open epics in priority order
body:
This ticket is the running roadmap. Open epics appear in the order
they should be tackled. As work shifts, the order shifts, and the
body is edited in place (`jjf update roadmap --body-file new.md`).

**This ticket never closes.**

# Current priority order (as of 2026-06-28, post-proptest-followup)

Above the line: shipping next. Below the line: queued behind the
shipping work.

1. **`epic:agent-ergonomics`** (`5a755ec`) — `ready`, `remember`,
   atomic claim, subagent protocol. The CLI verb set, the JSON
   contract, the sync loop, and the proptest harness are all in
   place. Remaining unfiled work in the epic body: the
   subagent-skill rewrite (current `subagent-working-a-jjforge-issue`
   skill is partially stale — references `git-bug` in places).

2. **`epic:host-asterinas-workspace`** (`ec5a0a8`) — sole open
   feature ticket sits here: `cc2fa96 host-asterinas-migrate`
   (Haiku fan-out import of ~968 markdown issues). The audit
   already landed; the migration is the real-world test-bed for
   "does jjforge actually work at scale as a planner for a project
   that isn't itself?"

3. **`epic:mvp-sync`** (`54caedd`) — feature-complete. Will only
   re-surface if a real consumer uncovers a gap.

4. **`epic:mvp-storage`** (`f21b950`) — feature-complete.

---

5. **`epic:multi-client`** (`99f01ed`) — sketched. Don't pull above
   the line until the work above closes.

6. **`epic:project-agent-orchestration`** (`585b84b`) — sketched,
   vision-level.

# Recently closed

- **`epic:mvp-cli`** (`a5f8122`) closed 2026-06-28. The
  "cli-replace-shim" entry on the prior roadmap was a phantom —
  the shim flip shipped during the 2026-06-22 cutover in commit
  `7371b179`. Current `bin/jjf` is a 30-line bash wrapper that
  picks the newer of `target/release/jjf` / `target/debug/jjf`
  and execs it; kept rather than dropped for the stale-binary
  guard and build-on-demand fallback.

- **`qa-proptest-followup`** cluster (5 tickets under
  `epic:agent-ergonomics`) closed 2026-06-28. Multi-issue
  generator + list-ready monotonicity + cycle rejection + memory
  surface + list_ids cardinality all shipped in one afternoon
  arc. Workspace test count 583 → 625; harness wall-clock ~28s
  release at default 16 cases. Two API contract surprises
  surfaced (`unset_memory` returns typed Invalid on missing key;
  `add_dep_edge` cycle rejection uses `DependencyCycle` /
  `SelfDependency`, not generic `Invalid`) and are now codified
  by property tests.

- **`epic:multi-project-readiness`** (`52ee82d`) closed 2026-06-25.
  Adversarial QA pass surfaced 13 findings; 11 children filed and
  resolved (10 fixed, 1 won't-fix). Multi-machine adoption now
  unblocked; sync round-trips exit 0; dep cycles rejected at
  preflight; concurrent comment retry budget absorbs realistic
  contention; corrupt refs surface as warnings; slug uniqueness
  spans all statuses; `jjf abandon` for mis-filed issues.

# Reader's contract

- The body above IS the truth. When priorities shift, edit the body
  via `jjf update roadmap --body-file <path>` and (optionally) drop
  a `jjf comment roadmap` announcing the change.
- For "what exists" — every issue, by label, by status — use the
  jjforge queries in `CLAUDE.md`, not a maintained index.
- Don't expand scope into epics below the line until this roadmap
  explicitly pulls them up.

# History

This ticket was re-filed in jjforge on 2026-06-22 during the git-bug
cutover (decision in archived git-bug ticket `d12031c` — see
`git-bug bug show d12031c` against `refs/bugs/*`). The pre-cutover
ticket lived at archived `6a65c0d`; its comment history (seven status
updates 2026-06-21 through 2026-06-22 carrying the priority changes
and the migration design call) is preserved in git refs and read via
`git-bug bug show 6a65c0d`.

The cutover doc at `docs/git-bug-cutover.md` is the bridge.
